items.title: Core views on AI safety: When, why, what, and how
items.summary: AI progress may lead to transformative AI systems in the next decade, but we do not yet understand how to make such systems safe and aligned with human values. In response, we are pursuing a variety of research directions aimed at better understanding, evaluating, and aligning AI systems.

items.title: News sites are the new newspapers: People are abandoning them for social media
items.summary: News sites are rapidly becoming the newspapers of the digital age. And you know what happened to newspapers. Worldwide, people in all age groups are ditching news sites and publishers’ apps in favor of social media and video networks. In fact, 18- to 34-year-olds are abandoning content on…

items.title: Base44 is one of the most-viewed brands on YouTube. Here’s a look at its ad strategy
items.summary: We chatted with Omer Shai, CMO of parent company Wix, about how the vibe-coding platform is looking to win over users in a crowded category.

items.title: KFC Rebrands to Help Lead ‘Chicken’s Next Chapter’
items.summary: Amid intensifying competition, KFC has updated its brand identity, menu, and restaurant design.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.summary: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

items.title: Disney is mostly sold out of ad inventory through Game 5 of NBA Finals
items.summary: Tonight’s Game 4 of the series between the Knicks and the Spurs has been sold out for weeks, a Disney Advertising exec said.

items.title: New York now requires disclosure of AI performers in ads. How will that change transparency in advertising?
items.summary: Marketers expect New York is a first mover and that disclosure may be a common practice soon. “My hope is that this is a table-setter that increases transparency,” one exec told us.

items.title: Why AT&T gave $1.2 million to first-time feature filmmakers
items.summary: The telecom company’s annual Untold Stories event at Tribeca Festival is designed to fund a marketable film project—and also get the brand in front of new audiences, one exec told us.

items.title: How soccer creators are helping to make the World Cup more than a moment
items.summary: Leagues like La Liga in Spain and Bundesliga in Germany are eager to hook American fans, and they’re using influencers to do it.

items.title: ‘The human touch’: Inside Etsy’s life-by-numbers brand campaign
items.summary: “As more of life becomes automated and impersonal, people are increasingly looking for things that feel more personal, expressive, and human,” the brand’s CMO told us.
